title: Matched-pair prompt record
description: Schema for a single prompt record in the tool-calibration matched-pair corpus. Each record must be addressable by stable id, sliceable on every analysis axis, and explicitly linked to its pair.

id.description: Globally unique prompt id, format `{tool}-{domain}-{difficulty}-{disambiguator}-{NNN}-{shortuuid}`. Fields are `-`-joined; within-field `_` is allowed (e.g. tool=`general_knowledge_lookup`). See id_scheme.py.

pair_id.description: Pair identifier shared by both halves of a matched pair. Identical to id minus the trailing `-shortuuid`.

condition.description: Which half of the matched comparison this record represents.

pair_type.description: A = difficulty manipulation (task changes, affordance constant). B = affordance manipulation (task constant, available tools differ).

tool_target.description: Which tool this prompt is designed to probe. `none` for control prompts that should not warrant any tool.

domain.description: Coarse topical bucket. Free-form; curators reuse-before-invent against the study-level canonical list at ../../canonical_domains.md.

sub_domain.description: Optional finer-grained slice nested within `domain` (never orthogonal). Free-form. Use when `domain` alone loses important structure.

value.description: Authoritative difficulty bucket for slicing. Initially set by llm_assessment.value at record creation; may be overridden by human_review.

model.description: Model id that proposed this label (e.g. claude-opus-4-7). Required: every record carries an LLM assessment at creation time.

value.description: The label the LLM originally proposed. Preserved even if the human overrides.

confidence.description: LLM's stated confidence in the label.

reasoning.description: Optional one-paragraph rationale from the LLM. Encouraged for `low` confidence.

agreed.description: True if the human accepts the LLM's proposed value. False if the top-level `value` was overridden from llm_assessment.value.

overridden_to.description: The new value the human chose, if agreed=false. Mirrors the top-level value; included here for human_review self-containment.

notes.description: Short prose, especially on overrides — what about the prompt made the human disagree with the LLM.

difficulty_label.description: Curator-assigned difficulty hypothesis. LLM-proposed at record creation, optionally human-reviewed. Slice by `difficulty_label.value`; mine `llm_assessment` vs `human_review` divergence as a separate research signal.

success_rate.description: Empirical fraction of trials the model succeeded on this prompt.

n.description: Number of trials backing the success_rate.

last_run.description: Date of the most recent calibration run for this (record, model) pair.

difficulty_calibrated.description: Empirical difficulty per target model, keyed by model id. Raw signal: success_rate + n. Bucketing into trivial/easy/medium/hard/extreme happens at analysis time using thresholds defined in the calibration methodology (planned A4).

frequency_class.description: Answer salience: how well-known is the fact or answer being probed. `common` = widely-known; `uncommon` = niche but findable; `edge` = obscure or at the periphery of plausible knowledge. NOT prompt-type frequency or tool-need frequency.

register_tone.description: Tone dimension of the user_prompt. Orthogonal to form and length.

register_form.description: Speech-act form of the user_prompt. Orthogonal to tone and length.

register_length.description: Length dimension of the user_prompt. Orthogonal to tone and form.

register_notes.description: Optional free-form nuance about register that the enum can't capture.

system_prompt_id.description: Pointer to a system prompt template in system_prompts/manifest.json.

token_counts.description: Token count of user_prompt under each tokenizer of interest, keyed by tokenizer id (e.g. `cl100k_base`, `claude-3-tokenizer`). Mirrors the per-model dict shape used by difficulty_calibrated. Optional; populated during corpus prep (planned A2/A3).

expected_tool_call.description: Whether a well-calibrated agent should call any tool on this prompt under the linked system_prompt_id.

expected_call_confidence.description: How confident the curator is in expected_tool_call. Low = the prompt sits at the calibration boundary deliberately.

expected_pair_behavior.description: One-line description of how this record should differ from its pair_id sibling. Used as a sanity check when reviewing pairs.

calibration_status.description: `assumed` until an empirical calibration run validates the difficulty. `verified` after at least one target-model run agrees. `contested` if calibration disagrees with the human label.

calibration_verified_on.description: Date the calibration was verified, if applicable.

source.description: Where this prompt came from. hand_curated = a human wrote it; llm_generated = produced in bulk generation (A3); transformed = derived from another record.

source_record_id.description: If source is `transformed`, the id of the parent record.
